answer:
verdict: not criminal, but real civil exposure — linkedin actively sues cos like urs & the biz can get killed by injunction/settlement. plan for it, dont assume "public data = safe"

breakdown:

**1. CFAA (fed hacking law)** — low risk IF truly public pages, no login. hiQ v linkedin (9th cir 2022) + van buren (scotus 2021): scraping public data w/o auth barriers ≠ CFAA violation. BUT if u use logged-in accts, fake accts, or dodge IP blocks after C&D, CFAA is back on table — hiQ ultimately got hit partly 4 fake-acct access to password-protected pages

**2. breach of contract — ur biggest real risk.** hiQ *lost* on this: court found scraping violated linkedin user agreement, ended w/ $500k judgment + hiQ basically died. if anyone at ur co has a linkedin acct (they do), u've accepted ToS banning scraping. even scraping logged-out, LI argues assent. this is the theory that wins 4 them

**3. state law + torts** — cal comp crime law (§502), trespass to chattels, misappropriation, unfair competition. all pled successfully vs hiQ

**4. privacy laws** — CCPA/CPRA + ~20 state laws: if u touch CA residents' data u likely qualify as data broker → must register (CA delete act = big deal, DROP deletion system live now), honor deletion/opt-outs, give notice. FTC also watching "surveillance pricing"/enrichment cos. fines per violation add up fast

**5. it's not theoretical** — LI sued proxycurl/nubela jan 2025 (settled, proxycurl shut down its LI product), + purged apollo.io & seamless.ai company pages in 2025 crackdown. enrichment-for-CRM is exactly the profile they target

**what changes ur exposure:**
- logged-in / fake accts / rotating proxies to evade blocks = way worse
- ignoring a C&D = way worse (revoked access strengthens their claims)
- buying data from 3rd-party scraper instead of scraping urself = shifts but doesn't eliminate risk (downstream use, deletion obligations)
- customers may also demand indemnification — check ur contracts, that's where $ exposure concentrates

**recs:** talk to actual counsel b4 scaling; register as data broker where req'd; build deletion pipeline (incl derived/enriched fields); no fake accts ever; have a C&D response plan; consider licensed data sources (people data labs etc still carry risk but less direct)

realistic worst case: C&D → lawsuit → injunction that kills core product + 6-7 fig settlement. criminal charges: very unlikely if u stay logged-out & public-only

Sources: [hiQ v. LinkedIn wrap-up (ZwillGen)](https://www.zwillgen.com/alternative-data/hiq-v-linkedin-wrapped-up-web-scraping-lessons-learned/), [hiQ Labs v. LinkedIn (Wikipedia)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HiQ_Labs_v._LinkedIn), [LinkedIn/hiQ proposed judgment (Privacy World)](https://www.privacyworld.blog/2022/12/linkedins-data-scraping-battle-with-hiq-labs-ends-with-proposed-judgment/), [Is scraping LinkedIn legal in 2026 (Nubela/Proxycurl)](https://nubela.co/blog/is-scraping-linkedin-legal-in-2026/), [Rulings on data scraping legality (Farella)](https://www.fbm.com/publications/what-recent-rulings-in-hiq-v-linkedin-and-other-cases-say-about-the-legality-of-data-scraping/)
